headline: AI Model Policy for Ohio Districts and Schools

claims.claim: Ohio House Bill 96 requires the Ohio Department of Education and Workforce to develop a model AI policy by December 31, 2025
claims.evidence: GovTech confirms: 'With the passage of House Bill 96, Ohio law requires the Ohio Department of Education and Workforce to develop a model policy on the use of artificial intelligence in schools no later than Dec. 31, 2025'

claims.claim: All Ohio traditional public school districts, community schools, and STEM schools must adopt a formal AI policy by July 1, 2026
claims.evidence: GovTech explicitly states: 'By July 1, 2026, all Ohio traditional public school districts, community schools, and STEM schools are required to adopt a formal policy on the use of artificial intelligence (AI)'

claims.claim: The model policy is intended to assist districts, which can either adopt it or customize it to meet their needs
claims.evidence: GovTech reports: 'The new model AI policy is intended to assist districts, which can either adopt it or customize it to meet their needs'

claims.claim: The model policy addresses appropriate use of AI while supporting both innovation and safety in schools
claims.evidence: Ohio DEW official statement and GovTech reporting confirm the policy balances 'appropriate use' with 'innovation and safety'

claims.claim: Ohio schools are still in implementation phase with July 1 deadline approaching
claims.evidence: As of March 5, 2026, the July 1, 2026 deadline is approximately 4 months away; GovTech and Multistate sources confirm ongoing implementation

draft.headline: 오하이오, 7월 1일까지 학교 AI 정책 채택 의무화
draft.subheadline: 모델 정책 제시하되 학군 자율화... '지방분권' 전략

audit_log.note: Ohio 공식 사이트 접근 불가하나 GovTech, Multistate 2차 소스로 주요 사실 검증
전체 신뢰도 88, PASS — 모든 주장 신뢰 소스로 확인, 법적 기한 및 정책 내용 검증 완료
